title: Thinking content capture on AssistantMessage
purpose: ThinkingDelta events from the provider are silently dropped in assembly.rs. The model's reasoning content is lost — it cannot be replayed, audited, or displayed by the TUI. This brief captures thinking content as a field on AssistantMessage, accumulates it during assembly, and threads it through session conversion so multi-turn conversations that echo thinking back to the provider work correctly.
task: Add a thinking field (String, not Option — empty string when no thinking produced) to AssistantMessage in session/events.rs. Accumulate ThinkingDelta text in assembly.rs alongside the existing text buffer. Add a thinking field to AssembledResponse. Thread thinking through classify.rs into the SessionEvent. Update events_to_messages in conversion.rs to include thinking content in the provider message when non-empty. Clean up zombie Option wrappers on AssistantMessage.content (change from Option<String> to String — empty string when no text produced) and remove the #[serde(default)] on usage since there are no existing serialised sessions to migrate.

requirements.title: Thinking field on AssistantMessage and AssembledResponse
requirements.spec: AssistantMessage SHALL carry accumulated thinking content as a String field. AssembledResponse SHALL carry accumulated thinking content as a String field.
acceptance: SessionEvent::AssistantMessage gains field: thinking: String (empty string when no thinking)
AssistantMessage.content changes from Option<String> to String (empty string when no text — no backwards compat needed)
#[serde(default)] removed from AssistantMessage.usage (no existing sessions to migrate)
All construction sites for AssistantMessage updated (events.rs tests, conversion.rs, classify.rs, fork.rs, any test files)
Test: AssistantMessage serde round-trip with thinking content
Test: AssistantMessage serde round-trip with empty thinking and empty content

requirements.title: ThinkingDelta accumulation in assembly.rs
requirements.spec: assemble_response SHALL accumulate ThinkingDelta text into the thinking field instead of discarding it.
acceptance: ThinkingDelta match arm at line 76 accumulates text into a thinking_buffer (same pattern as text buffer)
Text-only responses have empty thinking string
Thinking-only responses have empty text string
Mixed responses (thinking + text + tool calls) accumulate all three independently
Test: ThinkingDelta events produce non-empty thinking on AssembledResponse
Test: mixed ThinkingDelta + TextDelta events accumulate independently

requirements.title: Thinking threaded through classify.rs to SessionEvent
requirements.spec: The classification step SHALL thread thinking content from AssembledResponse into the SessionEvent::AssistantMessage.
acceptance: classify.rs (or wherever AssembledResponse is converted to SessionEvent) maps assembled.thinking to the AssistantMessage thinking field
Test: provider turn with ThinkingDelta produces SessionEvent::AssistantMessage with thinking populated

requirements.title: Thinking in events_to_messages conversion
requirements.spec: events_to_messages SHALL include thinking content in the provider message when non-empty, for multi-turn conversations where reasoning is echoed back.
acceptance: When AssistantMessage.thinking is non-empty, the converted Message includes thinking content (provider-specific handling — may need a thinking field on Message or a separate message)
When thinking is empty, conversion behaves exactly as before
Test: AssistantMessage with thinking produces a Message that preserves the thinking content

boundaries: SHALL NOT add Option wrappers for backwards compatibility — this crate has no existing serialised sessions
SHALL NOT modify the ProviderEvent enum — ThinkingDelta continues to flow through the broadcast channel unchanged
SHALL NOT modify how the TUI or print mode consume ProviderEvents — they subscribe to the broadcast channel independently
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: cargo test -p norn — all tests pass
ThinkingDelta events produce non-empty thinking on AssembledResponse — verified by test
AssistantMessage with thinking round-trips through serde — verified by test
events_to_messages preserves thinking content — verified by test
No Option<String> on AssistantMessage.content — verified by inspection
No #[serde(default)] on AssistantMessage.usage — verified by inspection
